extraction.one_line_summary: SKJELMOSE Mattias held yellow by improving exponentially through time checks, surviving AYUSO's 8-second stage win with rhythm acceleration in final 15km
tactical_patterns: SKJELMOSE showed exponential improvement between time checks rather than even pacing, catching rider 2min ahead by checkpoint 2
AYUSO took 8sec from EVENEPOEL in final 5km descent, suggesting elite descending skills differentiate young GC contenders
GALL Felix collapsed in time trial despite 8sec GC gap, confirming climber types vulnerable in mixed TT courses

rider_signals.SKJELMOSE Mattias: positive: exponential pacing strategy (improving between checks vs fading) reveals maturity beyond typical breakthrough season pattern
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: positive: 8sec gain on EVENEPOEL in final descent signals world-class TT descending, rare for 20-year-old in second race
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: set fastest time but AYUSO's descent gains suggest vulnerability when terrain turns technical at end
rider_signals.GALL Felix: negative: lost 46sec from 4th GC despite only 8sec deficit, exposing climber-only limitation in short mixed TTs
rider_signals.BISSEGGER Stefan: negative: 23.9sec down at first check despite being time trial specialist, suggesting early-season form issues
